javax.media.mscontrol.join
Interface JoinableStream

All Superinterfaces:
Joinable
public interface JoinableStream
extends Joinable

A JoinableStream represents a media stream channel (or port), of a given type: audio, video, etc. It is a Joinable and as such, can be used as an argument to join.
A JoinableStream has always one parent JoinableContainer, and that parent cannot change.
Operating on JoinableStreams instead of their JoinableContainer, enables stream-specific compositions, like muting only the video, or sending the video to a different object.

Nested Class Summary
static class JoinableStream.StreamType
          Can be audio, video, or message.
 
Nested classes/interfaces inherited from interface javax.media.mscontrol.join.Joinable
Joinable.Direction
 
Method Summary
 JoinableContainer getContainer()
          Returns the parent container of this stream
 JoinableStream.StreamType getType()
          Returns the enumerated type of the stream (audio, video, ...).

getType

getContainer
